title: Beatdown on Gendo
wikitext:
[]: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Fri Dec 25, 2020 6:42 pm In the latest trailer, Gendo's face has bruises and his visor sparks indicating it's damaged. Anyone else see this? If this ia Shinji laying the smackdown he seems way out of character aside from that punch he delivered to Toji.

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by Big Ben » Fri Dec 25, 2020 6:49 pm It kind of looks like he got shot with a gun through his visor.

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by andreh » Fri Dec 25, 2020 6:53 pm Yep, not only his visor, but it looks like his FACE is damaged as well: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Fri Dec 25, 2020 6:55 pm Are we going to see a situation like The Major from Hellsing... SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by Ghdust » Fri Dec 25, 2020 7:05 pm I think those are actually gunshot wounds, and judging by the background color of certain cuts in the trailer: I think either Ritsuko, Misato, or Midori (the pink haired chick) shot him

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by ShikinamiBogard » Fri Dec 25, 2020 7:15 pm Ghdust wrote: I think those are actually gunshot wounds, and judging by the background color of certain cuts in the trailer: I think either Misato, Ritsuko, or Midori (the pink haired chick) shot him

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Dec 25, 2020 7:28 pm I'm ready for Robo-Gendo.

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by Line » Fri Dec 25, 2020 7:52 pm The thing is, Shinji already tried (but failed) to beat the shit out of his father in the manga issue when Touji don't survive this time around. To me, Shinji towards Gendo always appeared as a tickling bomb. But honestly? I don't think a punch can left such marks. It look more like something exploded in his face...

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by Gus Hanson » Fri Dec 25, 2020 9:22 pm What would Midori of all people be doing there? Unless she were revealed to be a NERV spy operating under Gendo's command or something, you know me just reaching for the stars when it comes to analyzing...

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Dec 26, 2020 2:18 am Midori could be from a completely different scene, with the editing made to make us think that she and Ritsuko are both screaming at the same thing together. I'd guess someone shot Gendo in the visor, and the remaining scratches? I'm not sure. If those are bullets bouncing off Gendo's skull it means that Gendo really has gone full cyborg, Ghost in the Shell style.

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sat Dec 26, 2020 9:39 am I feel like something came out of Gendo's visor rather than him being shot in it. There's not any blood coming out of the hole and the marks on his face look more like scuffs or stains than the kind of splattering you'd expect from a gunshot. And look at the way the metal is puckered around the edge of the damaged portion of the visor. It's bubbled up around the hole and looks melted which isn't what you'd expect from a point of entry. Maybe he's learned the ancient art of eye beams?

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Dec 26, 2020 9:47 am Whichever scene it's from, Midori's rather nasty case of EoE-Face makes me concerned for her welfare.

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by Joseki » Sat Dec 26, 2020 10:02 am Since it's pretty much the exact same "scene" from the trailer we are discussing, I'm gonna quote myself from the Midori thread: Joseki wrote: I think Midori may actually be arguing with Misato and Ritsuko about letting Shinji pilot Unit 01, as she is frightened of him and what he can do inside an Evangelion. She may also be the one to have harmed (shot?) Misato. When Shinji is brought on the Wunder deck she seems to be the one more disturbed by his presence (her eyes are twitching while the rest of bunnies are perfectly still). She is also the one bridge bunny doubting Misato's decision to counterattack the most, as she doesn't want to die yet. She has a big distrust in Shinji and she also doesn't seem to blindly believe in Misato, so that would make sense to me. Shinji's aggressive reaction may not be to Gendo but to Midori/Ritsuko attacking Misato. My personal guess is that Misato, Ritsuko, Shinji and Midori are in one scene, Gendo is a red herring and in a completely different scene with a Rei clone and Fuytsuki.

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by Criomante » Sat Dec 26, 2020 10:03 am BlueBasilisk wrote: I feel like something came out of Gendo's visor rather than him being shot in it. There's not any blood coming out of the hole and the marks on his face look more like scuffs or stains than the kind of splattering you'd expect from a gunshot. And look at the way the metal is puckered around the edge of the damaged portion of the visor. It's bubbled up around the hole and looks melted which isn't what you'd expect from a point of entry. Maybe he's learned the ancient art of eye beams?

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sat Dec 26, 2020 10:07 am ^ I'm thinking it's a reaction to him using Nebuchadnezzar's Key, myself. Who knows what kind of side-effects that thing will have.

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 26, 2020 12:46 pm For me that looks more like something exploded or he got hit by a piece of debris across the face which left that bruise / broke the visor He will then remove the visor 'cause it's damaged & then we will find out something. There was a concept sketch that still showed him with his normal eyes underneath; I think it will be more of a "barrier symbolism" thing where we don't find out what he's really thinking/ his motivation until we see his eyes.

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by Tajai » Sat Dec 26, 2020 12:48 pm Kendrix wrote: For me that looks more like something exploded or he got hit by a piece of debris across the face which left that bruise / broke the visor He will then remove the visor 'cause it's damaged & then we will find out something. There was a concept sketch that still showed him with his normal eyes underneath; I think it will be more of a "barrier symbolism" thing where we don't find out what he's really thinking/ his motivation until we see his eyes.

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 26, 2020 2:36 pm Tajai wrote: Perhaps he got injured from the eventual defeat and crash of the Neo-Nerv ship?

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by Blockio » Sat Dec 26, 2020 6:56 pm BlueBasilisk wrote: ^ I'm thinking it's a reaction to him using Nebuchadnezzar's Key, myself. Who knows what kind of side-effects that thing will have.

[]: Re: Beatdown on Gendo             Quote Post by cyharding » Sat Dec 26, 2020 9:20 pm BlueBasilisk wrote: ^ I'm thinking it's a reaction to him using Nebuchadnezzar's Key, myself. Who knows what kind of side-effects that thing will have.
